title: Applied Mathematics Essentials
content_compressed:
Applied math provides the computational and analytical tools underpinning science and engineering. Core areas: LinAlg, calculus, DiffEq, probability/stats, and numerical methods.

## Linear Algebra

Vectors: dot product a¬∑b = |a||b|cosŒ∏ = Œ£a·µ¢b·µ¢ (scalar, measures projection/similarity). Cross product a√ób = |a||b|sinŒ∏ nÃÇ (vector, perpendicular to both, magnitude = parallelogram area). Orthogonal vectors: a¬∑b = 0.

Matrices: m√ón array, multiplication (AB)·µ¢‚±º = Œ£‚Çñ a·µ¢‚Çñb‚Çñ‚±º. Not commutative: AB ‚âÝ BA generally. Identity matrix I: AI = IA = A. Inverse A‚Åª¬π exists iff det(A) ‚âÝ 0. For 2√ó2: A‚Åª¬π = (1/det)[d,-b;-c,a]. Transpose: (AB)·µÄ = B·µÄA·µÄ. Orthogonal matrix: Q·µÄQ = I (columns are orthonormal).

Determinant: det(A) = ad-bc for 2√ó2. Properties: det(AB) = det(A)det(B), det(A·µÄ) = det(A), row swap changes sign, det = 0 iff singular. Geometric interpretation: absolute value = volume scaling factor of linear transformation.

Eigenvalues/vectors: Av = Œªv, find Œª from det(A-ŒªI) = 0 (characteristic polynomial). Eigenvectors span eigenspaces. Symmetric matrices: real eigenvalues, orthogonal eigenvectors. Spectral decomposition A = QŒõQ·µÄ. Applications: PCA (variance maximization), vibration modes, stability analysis (eigenvalues of Jacobian).

SVD: A = UŒ£V·µÄ where U,V orthogonal, Œ£ diagonal w/ singular values œÉ·µ¢ ‚â• 0. Always exists. Low-rank approx by truncating smallest œÉ·µ¢ (image compression, noise reduction, recommender systems). Pseudoinverse A‚Å∫ = VŒ£‚Å∫U·µÄ for least-squares solutions.

Linear systems Ax = b: Gaussian elimination O(n¬≥). Cramer's rule (theoretical, impractical for large n). Existence/uniqueness: rank(A) = rank([A|b]) for consistency, rank = n for unique solution. Overdetermined (m>n): least squares xÃÇ = (A·µÄA)‚Åª¬πA·µÄb minimizes ||Ax-b||¬≤.

## Calculus

Limits: Œµ-Œ¥ definition formal, L'H√¥pital's rule for 0/0 or ‚àû/‚àû indeterminate forms. Continuity: f continuous at a if lim(x‚Üía)f(x) = f(a). IVT: continuous f on [a,b] attains every value between f(a) and f(b).

Differentiation: power rule, product rule (fg)' = f'g + fg', quotient rule, chain rule dy/dx = (dy/du)(du/dx). Implicit differentiation when y not explicitly isolated. Applications: optimization (f'=0 and second derivative test), linear approximation f(a+Œîx) ‚âà f(a) + f'(a)Œîx, related rates.

Integration: FTC ‚Äî ‚à´‚Çê·µá f(x)dx = F(b)-F(a) where F'=f. Techniques: substitution (reverse chain rule), integration by parts ‚à´udv = uv - ‚à´vdu (LIATE priority), partial fractions (rational functions), trig substitution (‚àö(a¬≤-x¬≤) ‚Üí x=asinŒ∏). Improper integrals: convergence tests (comparison, limit comparison, p-test: ‚à´‚ÇÅ^‚àû x‚Åª·µñdx converges iff p>1).

Multivariable: partial derivatives ‚àÇf/‚àÇx (hold other vars constant). Gradient ‚àáf = (‚àÇf/‚àÇx, ‚àÇf/‚àÇy, ‚àÇf/‚àÇz) points in direction of steepest ascent. Directional derivative D·µ§f = ‚àáf¬∑√ª. Critical points: ‚àáf = 0, classify w/ Hessian matrix (second partial derivative test). Lagrange multipliers for constrained optimization: ‚àáf = Œª‚àág at extrema of f subject to g=0.

Vector calculus: divergence ‚àá¬∑F (scalar, source/sink measure), curl ‚àá√óF (vector, rotation measure). Line integral ‚à´C F¬∑dr. Green's theorem: ‚àÆC F¬∑dr = ‚à¨D (‚àÇQ/‚àÇx - ‚àÇP/‚àÇy)dA. Stokes' theorem: ‚àÆC F¬∑dr = ‚à¨S (‚àá√óF)¬∑dS. Divergence theorem: ‚àØS F¬∑dS = ‚à≠V (‚àá¬∑F)dV.

## Differential Equations

First-order: separable dy/dx = g(x)h(y) ‚Üí ‚à´dy/h(y) = ‚à´g(x)dx. Linear dy/dx + P(x)y = Q(x) ‚Üí integrating factor Œº = e^(‚à´P dx). Exact: M(x,y)dx + N(x,y)dy = 0 where ‚àÇM/‚àÇy = ‚àÇN/‚àÇx.

Second-order linear constant coeff: ay'' + by' + cy = 0. Characteristic equation ar¬≤ + br + c = 0. Two distinct real roots ‚Üí y = c‚ÇÅe^(r‚ÇÅx) + c‚ÇÇe^(r‚ÇÇx). Repeated root ‚Üí y = (c‚ÇÅ + c‚ÇÇx)e^(rx). Complex roots Œ±¬±Œ≤i ‚Üí y = e^(Œ±x)(c‚ÇÅcos(Œ≤x) + c‚ÇÇsin(Œ≤x)). Nonhomogeneous: y = yh + yp, find yp via undetermined coefficients or variation of parameters.

Systems: dx/dt = Ax. Solution involves eigenvalues of A. Stability: all eigenvalues have Re(Œª) < 0 ‚Üí asymptotically stable. Phase portraits: node (real eigenvalues same sign), saddle (opposite signs), spiral (complex eigenvalues), center (purely imaginary).

PDEs: wave equation utt = c¬≤uxx, heat equation ut = Œ±uxx, Laplace equation ‚àá¬≤u = 0. Separation of variables: assume u(x,t) = X(x)T(t), get two ODEs. Fourier series for periodic BC: f(x) = a‚ÇÄ/2 + Œ£(a‚Çôcos(nœÄx/L) + b‚Çôsin(nœÄx/L)).

## Probability & Statistics

Probability axioms: P(Œ©)=1, P(A)‚â•0, P(A‚à™B) = P(A)+P(B) if disjoint. Conditional: P(A|B) = P(A‚à©B)/P(B). Bayes' theorem: P(A|B) = P(B|A)P(A)/P(B). Independence: P(A‚à©B) = P(A)P(B).

Distributions: Bernoulli (single trial p), Binomial (n trials, P(X=k) = C(n,k)p^k(1-p)^(n-k)), Poisson (rare events, P(X=k) = e‚ÅªŒªŒª^k/k!, mean=var=Œª). Normal N(Œº,œÉ¬≤): 68-95-99.7 rule. CLT: sample mean distribution ‚Üí normal as n‚Üí‚àû regardless of population distribution. Standard error SE = œÉ/‚àön.

Hypothesis testing: H‚ÇÄ (null) vs H‚ÇÅ (alternative). Test statistic ‚Üí p-value = P(data as extreme | H‚ÇÄ true). Reject H‚ÇÄ if p < Œ± (typically 0.05). Type I error (false positive) = Œ±, Type II error (false negative) = Œ≤, Power = 1-Œ≤. z-test (known œÉ), t-test (unknown œÉ, df=n-1), chi-square (categorical data), ANOVA (comparing >2 means).

Regression: y = Œ≤‚ÇÄ + Œ≤‚ÇÅx + Œµ. OLS minimizes Œ£Œµ·µ¢¬≤. R¬≤ = 1 - SSres/SStotal (proportion of variance explained). Check assumptions: linearity, independence, normality of residuals, equal variance (homoscedasticity). Multiple regression: y = XŒ≤ + Œµ, Œ≤ÃÇ = (X·µÄX)‚Åª¬πX·µÄy. Regularization: Ridge (L2, shrinks coefficients), LASSO (L1, sparse solutions).

## Numerical Methods

Root finding: bisection (guaranteed convergence, slow O(log n)), Newton-Raphson x‚Çô‚Çä‚ÇÅ = x‚Çô - f(x‚Çô)/f'(x‚Çô) (quadratic convergence near root, needs good initial guess, may diverge). Secant method: avoids derivative computation.

Numerical integration: trapezoidal rule (error O(h¬≤)), Simpson's rule (error O(h‚Å¥), requires even number of intervals). Gaussian quadrature: optimal node placement, exact for polynomials up to degree 2n-1 w/ n nodes.

ODE solvers: Euler's method y‚Çô‚Çä‚ÇÅ = y‚Çô + hf(t‚Çô,y‚Çô) ‚Äî first-order, simple but inaccurate. RK4 (Runge-Kutta 4th order): four slope evaluations per step, error O(h‚Å¥), workhorse method. Adaptive step size (RK45) adjusts h to maintain error tolerance. Stiff systems require implicit methods (backward Euler, BDF).

Interpolation: Lagrange polynomial (unique degree-n polynomial through n+1 points). Runge phenomenon: high-degree polynomial interpolation oscillates at edges ‚Üí use Chebyshev nodes or splines. Cubic splines: piecewise cubic, C¬≤ continuity, natural BC (S''=0 at endpoints).
